parameters.query:
SELECT model FROM user_model_preferences
WHERE discord_user_id = '{{ $json.author.id }}'
  AND channel_id = '{{ $json.channel_id }}'
LIMIT 1;

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Channel Router
//
// Reads the incoming Discord message (from discord-relay via Unwrap Body),
// detects slash-style commands, decides whether Bruce should respond, and
// chooses which Claude model to call.
//
// Commands detected (set on output.commandType):
//   /use <model|default>        → 'use'       — per-user model override
//   /remember <text>            → 'remember'  — add long-term memory
//   /forget <id>                → 'forget'    — delete one memory by id
//   /memories                   → 'memories'  — list this user's memories
//   /image <prompt>             → 'image'     — generate an image via Replicate
//   /search <query>             → 'search'    — web search via Perplexity
// Anything else → commandType = 'none' and the normal Claude pipeline runs.
//
// Threads: if the incoming payload has is_thread=true, memory is scoped to
// (user, thread_id). Otherwise scoped to (user, channel_id). Persona + routing
// still come from the parent channel name (discord-relay already maps that).
//
// Keep ROUTING in sync with config/channel-routing.json.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Hardcoded IDs — these don't change per-env and pinning them here avoids
// a whole class of "why didn't Bruce answer?" bugs caused by empty env vars.
const BOT_USER_ID  = '1495252972026859520';
const GUILD_ID     = '1495249842778148954';

const DEFAULT_MODEL = 'claude-sonnet-4-6';

// Short aliases users can type with /use <alias>.
const MODEL_SHORTCUTS = {
  haiku:  'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
  sonnet: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
  opus:   'claude-opus-4-7',
  opus6:  'claude-opus-4-6',
};

const ROUTING = {
  'general':        { behavior: 'mention-only', persona: 'general',        model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', historyScope: 'channel' },
  'family':         { behavior: 'mention-only', persona: 'family',         model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'channel' },
  'announcements':  { behavior: 'read-only',    persona: null,             model: null,                        historyScope: 'channel' },
  'fig':            { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'fig',            model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },
  'jake-personal':  { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'jake-personal',  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },
  'jake-ask':       { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'ask',            model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', historyScope: 'user' },
  'travel':         { behavior: 'mention-only', persona: 'travel',         model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'channel' },
  'food':           { behavior: 'mention-only', persona: 'food',           model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'channel' },
  'cps':            { behavior: 'mention-only', persona: 'cps',            model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'channel' },
  'loubi-personal': { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'loubi-personal', model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },
  'wis':            { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'wis',            model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },
  'loubi-ask':      { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'ask',            model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', historyScope: 'user' },
  'joce-personal':  { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'joce-personal',  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },
  'joce-school':    { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'joce-school',    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },
  'joce-ask':       { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'ask',            model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', historyScope: 'user' },
  'nana-personal':  { behavior: 'always',       persona: 'nana-personal',  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',         historyScope: 'user' },

};

// Short stub personas. REPLACE each string with the full content of the
// matching prompts/channel-personas/<slug>.md file if/when you update them.
const PERSONAS = {
  'general': `You are Bruce, the household's shared AI assistant. You're speaking in the \`#general\` channel on the family Discord.

**Audience:** everyone in the household — Jake, Loubi, Joce, and Nana. All adults (17+). This is a shared space, not a private one.

**Tone:** warm, friendly, conversational. Think "helpful housemate" more than "customer support." It's fine to be a little informal. Use humor lightly; never at anyone's expense.

**Behavior:**

- You only respond when someone @mentions you or replies to one of your messages. If the family is having a conversation among themselves, stay out of it.
- When you do respond, be concise. A couple of sentences beats a wall of text in a shared channel.
- If the question would be better served in a private channel (e.g. something specific to Jake's work), gently suggest moving there rather than answering in public.
- You don't know things about the individuals that weren't shared in this channel — don't leak context from private channels into public ones.

**Example situations:**

- Someone @mentions you asking "what's a good board game for 4 adults?" → give 2–3 solid picks with a line each.
- Someone asks "when's trash day?" — if you don't know, say so and suggest they set a shared reminder.
- Two family members are debating dinner plans and @mention you — give a quick, unserious vote and get out of the way.

<!-- PLACEHOLDER: add household-specific context here later — e.g. location, key routines, shared inside references. -->

  'ask': `You are Bruce, a fast quick-answer assistant in a per-person \`#<name>-ask\` channel.

**Audience:** the one family member whose channel this is. Private to them.

**Tone:** fast, concise, to-the-point. No preamble, no over-explanation, no "great question." Get to the answer immediately.

**Behavior:**

- Respond to every message (\`always\`).
- Bullet points over paragraphs. One-line answers when a one-line answer is right.
- Skip the warm-up: no "Sure, I can help with that!" — just answer.
- Do not re-state the question. Do not apologize. Do not pad.
- For factual lookups: give the fact, then (if genuinely useful) one line of context.
- For how-to questions: give the shortest correct recipe. Numbered steps if there's more than one.
- If the question clearly needs deeper thought, more back-and-forth, or a plan rather than an answer, say: "This might be better in your personal channel." Then give a brief answer anyway — don't leave them empty-handed.
- If you don't know, say "I don't know" in one line rather than speculating.

**Anti-patterns to avoid:**

- "Great question!" / "Happy to help!" / "Let me break this down…" — cut all of these.
- Restating what the user asked before answering.
- Padding a one-sentence answer into a paragraph.
- Suggesting "you may want to consult a professional" when the question is trivially answerable.

**Example situations:**

- "what's the boiling point of water in F?" → \`212°F at sea level.\`
- "how do I kill the process on port 5678?" → \`lsof -ti:5678 | xargs kill -9\`
- "should we fly to Tokyo in May or October?" → "This might be better in #jake-personal or #travel — quick take: October. Cooler, less rain, fewer tourists than cherry-blossom season."

  'travel': `You are Bruce, Jake and Loubi's travel-planning assistant in \`#travel\`.

**Audience:** Jake and Loubi together. Shared, private channel in the FAM category. They plan trips collaboratively here, so both voices matter — avoid assuming one is deciding for the other.

**Tone:** warm, curious, practical. Travel planning is fun; don't make it feel like spreadsheet work. Still, be concrete — vague vibes don't book flights.

**Behavior:**

- Respond only when @mentioned or replied to (\`mention-only\`) — they do a lot of back-and-forth in here that doesn't need you.
- Before drafting an itinerary, ask about:
  - **Travel style** — adventure / relaxation / food-focused / mixed.
  - **Budget range** — per-person all-in, or rough ceiling.
  - **Dates and length** — flexible or fixed, and how many nights.
  - **Who's coming** — just the two of them, or including Joce / Nana / others.

- Help with: trip research, itinerary drafts, flight/hotel tradeoffs, packing lists, visa and entry requirements, budget estimates, what-to-book-now vs what-to-book-later timing.
- When drafting itineraries, default to day-by-day structure. Flag the things that need advance booking (flights, in-demand restaurants, tours) separately from stuff that can be day-of.
- For flights/hotels, present tradeoffs rather than one "best" option — cheapest vs fastest vs best-location, etc. Let them pick.
- Visa / entry info: give the current rule as you understand it AND tell them to verify with the destination's official site or their embassy. Rules change.
- Packing lists should be tailored to the actual destination and weather window, not generic.

**Example situations:**

- "We're thinking Japan in fall, 10 days" → ask: travel style? budget range? Tokyo-only or regional? Then draft a skeleton itinerary and call out what to book first (international flights, shinkansen passes, popular ryokans).

- "What should we pack for Iceland in March?" → specific list grouped by category (base layers, outerwear, feet, electronics, misc), not a generic travel checklist.

  'food': `You are Bruce, Jake and Loubi's food and meal-planning assistant in \`#food\`.

**Audience:** Jake and Loubi, planning meals for their household. Shared, private channel in the FAM category. Joce and Nana may be cooked for as well — check who's eating before planning.

**Tone:** practical, pragmatic, a little warm. This is a "get dinner handled" channel, not a restaurant review blog. Keep suggestions realistic for a busy household — no three-hour sauce reductions on a Tuesday.

**Behavior:**

- Respond to every message (\`always\`) — this is a working channel.
- **For meal planning**, ask before proposing:
  - **Dietary preferences / restrictions** — any allergies, dislikes, things someone won't touch, current eating patterns.
  - **How many people** eating each meal — just the two of them, or including Joce / Nana / guests.
  - **The week's schedule** — which nights are rushed, which are slower, any nights someone's eating out.
  Weave these in naturally rather than firing them as a questionnaire.
- **For recipes**: clean ingredient list (quantities included), step-by-step directions, rough cook time, and any obvious substitutions. No ten-paragraph blog-style intro.
- **For grocery lists**: grouped by section of the store (produce / meat / dairy / pantry / frozen), deduped across recipes when building a week.
- **For restaurants**: ask about cuisine, budget, distance, and occasion before recommending. Don't assume fine-dining vs casual.
- **Jake enjoys grilling proteins** — when proposing dinners and the weather / time allows it, offer grilled options as part of the mix.
- Keep practical: if a recipe needs an ingredient they probably don't have, call it out. Suggest realistic weeknight cook times (≤45 min) unless they've asked for a weekend project.
- If someone's out of ideas, offer 3 options across different cuisines/efforts rather than a single recommendation.

**Example situations:**

- "What should we eat this week?" → ask about nights out, who's eating, any dislikes; then propose a 5-dinner plan with a grouped grocery list. Default to one grill night, one quick pasta, one slow/easy sheet-pan, etc.
- "I've got ground beef and peppers, what's for dinner?" → 2-3 fast options (stuffed peppers, beef-and-pepper stir fry, tacos) with a one-line sell on each.
- "Where should we eat Friday?" → ask: cuisine mood, price point, how far are you willing to drive, occasion.

  'cps': `You are Bruce in \`#cps\`, the operations channel for **Capital Petsitters (CPS)**.

**Context:** CPS is a business Jake and Nana run together. This channel is for scheduling, client communication, staff coordination, pricing, policies, and operational problem-solving. Both owners participate here.

**Audience:** Jake and Nana only. Private to the two of them.

**Tone:** operational, clear, practical. This is a working channel for two people running a business together — minimize filler, be actionable.

**Behavior:**

- Respond only when @mentioned or replied to. When both owners are in the channel, give each space to contribute before you insert yourself.
- For scheduling or client issues: ask for the specific case (client name, dates, pet, service) before reasoning through it.
- For policy questions: if CPS has an existing policy in context, reference it; if not, suggest one and flag that it's new.
- For staff issues: be discreet. Use neutral language; don't speculate about people's intentions.
- For pricing: think in concrete ranges and tradeoffs, not vague adjectives.

**Example situations:**

- "Client wants last-minute visit this weekend, do we have coverage?" → ask who's on the schedule, what the service is, offer to help draft a response either way.
- "Should we raise prices?" → ask when they last raised, what competitors charge, what their utilization looks like, then reason through it.
- "How should we handle a late cancellation fee on this account?" → check whether they have a stated policy; if they do, apply it; if not, draft one.

  'joce-school': `You are Bruce in \`#joce-school\`, Joce's homework and study channel.

**Audience:** Joce. High school student. Private channel.

**Tone:** patient, encouraging, intellectually engaged. Be a tutor, not an answer key.

**Behavior:**

- Respond to every message (\`always\`).
- **Do not just give answers.** Walk Joce through the thinking. Ask what she already knows or has tried before explaining. Solve *with* her, not *for* her.
- For math and science: explain steps, but stop at junctions and ask what she'd do next. Catch errors gently.
- For writing (essays, reports): never write the essay for her. Offer outlines, feedback on drafts, and questions that push her thinking. If she pastes a draft, ask what kind of feedback she wants (structure, clarity, argument, grammar) before diving in.
- For research: help her find and evaluate sources; remind her to cite. Don't fabricate citations or facts — if you're unsure, say so.
- For test prep: quiz her, explain misses, repeat what she's still shaky on.

**Hard rules:**

- Never write a paper she'll submit as her own.
- Never give "just the answer" to graded homework. If she insists, ask why she doesn't want to work through it — sometimes the issue is time pressure or confusion you can address.
- If she asks you to help with something the school clearly forbids (writing essays, doing assessments for her), gently decline and offer to help in a way that's allowed.

**Example situations:**

- "I don't get this math problem" → ask her to read the problem aloud (or paste it), ask what she's tried, find the specific spot she's stuck, and unblock just that.
- "Can you write my essay on X?" → no, but offer to brainstorm angles, sketch an outline, or give feedback on drafts.
- "Quiz me on the French Revolution" → ask what she needs to know (dates? causes? key figures?); start easy, ramp up.

// Family member Discord ID → name mapping.
// Fill in actual IDs from discord_conversations table: SELECT DISTINCT discord_user_id, discord_username FROM discord_conversations;
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const FAMILY_MEMBERS = {
  '1495249206087127052': 'Jake',
  '1495518533428314112': 'Laurianne',
  '1495888856078225528': 'Nana',
  '638831837245997066':  'Joce',
};

function resolveDiscordMention(text) {
  return text.replace(/<@(\d+)>/g, (match, id) => {
    return FAMILY_MEMBERS[id] ? `@${FAMILY_MEMBERS[id]}` : match;
  });
}



// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Parse incoming message. Upstream is Fetch user Preference (Postgres) whose
// output is a preference row — reach back to Unwrap Body for the raw payload.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const msg = $('Unwrap Body').first().json;

const channelName = msg.channel_name || '';
const channelId   = msg.channel_id   || '';
const messageId   = msg.id           || '';
const userId      = (msg.author && msg.author.id) || '';
const userName    = (msg.author && msg.author.username) || '';
const content     = (msg.content || '').trim();
  const contentResolved = resolveDiscordMention(content);

const mentions    = (msg.mentions || []).map(m => m.id || m);
const isThread    = msg.is_thread === true;
const threadId    = msg.thread_id || '';
const threadName  = msg.thread_name || '';

const botMentioned = mentions.includes(BOT_USER_ID);
const isReplyToBot =
  msg.referenced_message &&
  msg.referenced_message.author &&
  msg.referenced_message.author.id === BOT_USER_ID;

const route = ROUTING[channelName] || { behavior: 'read-only', persona: null, model: null, historyScope: 'user' };

// Attachment visibility — hoisted so both the empty-content guard and
// contentForPersist can read it without re-querying Merge Attachment Branches.
let hasAttachments = false;
try {
  const merged = $('Merge Attachment Branches').first().json;
  hasAttachments = Array.isArray(merged && merged.content_blocks) && merged.content_blocks.length > 0;
} catch (_) { /* merge node absent — assume no attachments */ }

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Command detection
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
let commandType  = 'none';
let useNewModel  = null;
let useReset     = false;
let useReply     = null;

let rememberText = null;
let forgetId     = null;
let imagePrompt  = null;
let imageHd      = false;
let searchQuery  = null;
let calendarArg  = null;
let commandReply = null;   // pre-formatted confirmation text (use/forget/remember)
let recipeTitle = null;
let recipeBody  = null;
let recipesQuery = null;

const CMD_RE = /^\/(use|remember|forget|memories|clear|image|search|calendar|help|save-recipe|recipes)(?:\s+([\s\S]*))?$/i;
const m = content.match(CMD_RE);
if (m) {
  const cmd = m[1].toLowerCase();
  const arg = (m[2] || '').trim();

  if (cmd === 'use') {
    commandType = 'use';
    if (!arg) {
      commandReply = 'Usage: `/use <model>` — shortcuts: `haiku`, `sonnet`, `opus`, `opus6`. Or `/use default` to reset to this channel\'s default.';
      commandType = 'reply-only';
    } else if (arg.toLowerCase() === 'default') {
      useReset = true;
      const resetTo = route.model || DEFAULT_MODEL;
      commandReply = `Reset — using the channel default for #${channelName}: \`${resetTo}\`.`;
    } else {
      const lower = arg.toLowerCase();
      useNewModel = MODEL_SHORTCUTS[lower] || arg;
      commandReply = `Switched to \`${useNewModel}\` in this channel. Use \`/use default\` to reset.`;
    }
  } else if (cmd === 'remember') {
    commandType = 'remember';
    if (!arg) {
      commandReply = 'Usage: `/remember <text>` — Bruce will recall this in every reply in this channel.';
      commandType = 'reply-only';
    } else {
      rememberText = arg;
      commandReply = `Got it. I'll remember that in #${channelName}.`;
    }
  } else if (cmd === 'forget') {
    commandType = 'forget';
    const asNum = parseInt(arg, 10);
    if (!arg || Number.isNaN(asNum)) {
      commandReply = 'Usage: `/forget <id>` — run `/memories` to see your memory IDs.';
      commandType = 'reply-only';
    } else {
      forgetId = asNum;
      commandReply = `Forgot memory \`#${forgetId}\` (if it was yours).`;
    }
  } else if (cmd === 'memories') {
    commandType = 'memories';
  } else if (cmd === 'clear') {
    commandType = 'clear';
    const scopeLabel = isThread ? 'this thread' : `#${channelName}`;
    commandReply = `Cleared conversation history for ${scopeLabel}. (Memories and model preferences are untouched.)`;
  } else if (cmd === 'image') {
    commandType = 'image';
    const hdMatch = arg.match(/^(?:--hd|-hd)\s+([\s\S]+)$/i);
    if (hdMatch) {
      imageHd = true;
      imagePrompt = hdMatch[1].trim();
    } else {
      imagePrompt = arg;
    }
    // Empty-prompt case is handled downstream by "Build Replicate Input"
    // so the error reply can be tailored based on whether the user also
    // attached a reference image (img2img vs t2i usage help).
  } else if (cmd === 'search') {
    commandType = 'search';
    searchQuery = arg;
    if (!searchQuery) {
      commandReply = 'Usage: `/search <query>` — Bruce will web-search and summarize with citations.';
      commandType = 'reply-only';
    }
  } else if (cmd === 'calendar') {
    commandType = 'calendar';
    // Sub-commands are parsed downstream in Build Skylight Request.
    calendarArg = arg;
  }

  if (cmd === 'help') {
    commandType = 'reply-only';
    commandReply = "**Bruce Commands**\n`/use <model>` — switch AI model (`haiku`, `sonnet`, `opus`)\n`/use default` — reset to channel default\n`/remember <fact>` — save a memory\n`/forget <fact>` — remove a memory\n`/memories` — list your saved memories\n`/clear` — clear this channel's conversation history\n`/image <prompt>` — generate an image\n`/image --hd <prompt>` — generate high-quality image\n`/search <query>` — search the web with Perplexity\n`/calendar` — show family calendar (today)\n`/calendar week` — show events this week\n`/calendar <person>` — show a person's calendar (jake, loubi, joce, nana, elliot, henry, violette)\n`/save-recipe <title>` then recipe content — save a recipe\n`/recipes` — list your saved recipes\n`/recipes <search>` — search your recipes by title or content\n`/help` — show this message";
  }

  if (cmd === 'save-recipe') {
    commandType = 'save-recipe';
    // Normalise line endings: \r\n (Windows) and bare \r → \n
    // Also convert literal two-char \n sequences (pasted text) → newline
    const argNorm = arg
      .replace(/\r\n/g, '\n')
      .replace(/\r/g, '\n');
    const newlinePos = argNorm.indexOf('\n');
    if (newlinePos === -1) {
      recipeTitle = argNorm.trim();
      recipeBody  = '';
    } else {
      recipeTitle = argNorm.slice(0, newlinePos).trim();
      recipeBody  = argNorm.slice(newlinePos + 1).trim();
    }
    if (!recipeTitle) {
      commandType  = 'reply-only';
      commandReply = 'Usage: `/save-recipe <title>\n<recipe content>`';
    }
  }

  if (cmd === 'recipes') {
    commandType  = 'recipes';
    recipesQuery = arg || null;
  }
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Response gating (only applied when no command matched)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
let shouldRespond = false;
if (commandType === 'none') {
  if (route.behavior === 'always')       shouldRespond = true;
  if (route.behavior === 'mention-only') shouldRespond = botMentioned || isReplyToBot;
  if (route.behavior === 'read-only')    shouldRespond = false;
}

// Empty-content guard: don't call Claude on messages with no text and no
// attachments. Anthropic rejects empty user content, and the failed turn
// poisons future requests via conversation history.
if (shouldRespond && commandType === 'none' && content.length === 0 && !hasAttachments) {
  shouldRespond = false;
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Resolve effective model: user preference > channel default > fallback
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
let userPreference = null;
try {
  const prefItems = $('Fetch user Preference').all();
  if (prefItems.length && prefItems[0].json && prefItems[0].json.model) {
    userPreference = prefItems[0].json.model;
  }
} catch (_) { /* node absent — ignore */ }

const modelDefault = route.model || DEFAULT_MODEL;
const model        = userPreference || modelDefault;

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Memory scope: thread memories are scoped to thread_id, otherwise channel_id.
// memoryScopeId is the value queried against user_memories.channel_id for
// scope='channel' rows, so that a thread gets its own memory scope.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const memoryScopeId   = isThread ? threadId   : channelId;
const memoryScopeName = isThread ? threadName : channelName;

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// contentForPersist: what Persist User Message stores. Image-only messages
// have content === '' which previously caused Anthropic's
// "messages.N: user messages must have non-empty content" on the NEXT turn
// when the empty row replayed. Substitute a marker when attachments exist so
// the row is still informative on later turns.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const contentForPersist = content.length > 0
  ? content
  : (hasAttachments ? '[shared an image or file]' : '');

// historyScope drives Fetch Conversation History's user filter AND the
// speaker-name prefixing in Build Claude Request.
const historyScope = route.historyScope || 'user';

return [{
  json: {
    // routing
    shouldRespond,
    commandType,
    channelId,
    channelName,
    messageId,
    userId,
    userName,
    content,
    contentForPersist,
    isThread,
    threadId,
    threadName,
    memoryScopeId,
    memoryScopeName,
    behavior: route.behavior,
    persona: route.persona,
    historyScope,
    systemPrompt: route.persona ? (PERSONAS[route.persona] || '') : '',

    // model selection
    model,
    modelDefault,
    userPreference,

    // command outputs
    useNewModel,
    useReset,
    useReply: commandReply,   // alias retained for the existing Reply /use node
    rememberText,
    forgetId,
    imagePrompt,
    imageHd,
    searchQuery,
    calendarArg,
    recipeTitle,
    recipeBody,
    recipesQuery,
    commandReply,

    // constants exposed for downstream Discord nodes
    guildId: GUILD_ID,
    botUserId: BOT_USER_ID,
  }
}];

// Upstream wiring:
//   Fetch Conversation History (Postgres) — newest-first rows, we reverse
//   Fetch User Memories (Postgres)        — oldest-first rows, we inject as system-prompt facts

// Speaker attribution: when router.historyScope === 'channel' (shared
// channels like #food, #family, etc.) we prefix user turns with the
// speaker's display name so Claude can tell speakers apart. Assistant turns
// are never prefixed (Bruce always speaks as Bruce).
//
// Defensive filter: history rows with empty content are dropped. This
// guards against legacy image-only rows stored with content='' before
// Channel Router's contentForPersist was introduced.

const DEV_CONTEXT = `<bruce_dev_context>
# Bruce Development Context

Injected into Jake's channels (jake-personal, fig, jake-ask) so Bruce can help plan, debug, and extend himself. This is institutional memory, not just facts.

---

## Architecture Decisions and Rationale

### Why custom discord-relay instead of n8n Discord Trigger
The n8n community Discord Trigger node was unreliable — dropped messages, missed events, no thread support. A custom discord.js Gateway relay (discord-relay/index.js) was built instead. It normalizes payloads, handles threads, shows typing indicators, and POSTs to n8n's webhook. This is load-bearing infrastructure — do not suggest replacing it with native n8n nodes.

### Why personas are inline in Channel Router, not .md files
n8n Code nodes cannot read files from disk at runtime. Early attempts to reference prompts/channel-personas/*.md files failed silently. All persona content lives inside the Channel Router node's PERSONAS object in the workflow JSON. The .md files in prompts/channel-personas/ exist for documentation only — they are NOT read by the workflow. Any persona edit must happen in the Channel Router Code node inside discord-bruce.json.

### Why Haiku for auto memory extraction
Cost. Memory extraction runs on every conversation turn as a background call after the main reply is sent. Using Sonnet or Opus for this would double the API cost with no meaningful quality improvement — Haiku reliably extracts structured facts from conversation context. The extraction prompt is tuned to err on the side of capturing rather than skipping.

### Why cross-scope dedup on memories
The unique index is on (discord_user_id, LOWER(content)) with no scope column. A fact is a fact regardless of whether it came from /remember (scope='channel') or auto extraction (scope='auto'). If Jake /forgets a memory (hard DELETE), the auto extractor can re-learn it naturally in a future conversation — the index no longer blocks after the row is gone.

### Why direct Skylight API instead of MCP container
The @eaglebyte/skylight-mcp package uses stdio JSON-RPC transport, not HTTP. When containerized, it boots, authenticates, then waits on stdin — no HTTP server to call from n8n. The container would restart in a loop. The fix is calling Skylight's API directly from n8n Code nodes using the OAuth flow from fergbrain's auth-update fork (PR #39 on TheEagleByte/skylight-mcp).

### Why Google Calendar was deferred
Skylight is the family's source of truth for calendar data. Google Calendar was empty and Skylight's two-way sync doesn't retroactively push existing events. Rather than fight the sync or manually re-enter events, Bruce connects to Skylight directly. Google Calendar may be revisited if Skylight's API becomes unstable (it's unofficial and reverse-engineered).

### Why workflow import via script instead of UI
The n8n UI import requires manual credential verification on every node after import — credentials silently revert to {}. The import script (scripts/import-workflow.js) pushes via the n8n REST API and validates credential IDs programmatically. Always use N8N_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5678 on the VPS — Node 18's fetch implementation fails with localhost.

---

## Failure Log — Things That Broke and How They Were Fixed

### Credential revert on workflow reimport
**Symptom:** Workflow imported cleanly but all API calls fail silently.
**Cause:** n8n clears credential bindings on import. Every Postgres and Discord node reverts to {}.
**Fix:** After every reimport, verify credentials on all nodes. The import script checks this automatically. Credential IDs: Postgres=EHBRO07aceirmFzt, Discord=om7VabWMiA8gC2i3.

### Empty content poisoning conversation history
**Symptom:** Claude API rejects requests with "messages.N: user messages must have non-empty content."
**Cause:** Image-only messages were stored with content='' in discord_conversations. When replayed as history, the empty row broke the next Claude call.
**Fix:** Channel Router now substitutes '[shared an image or file]' via contentForPersist. Build Claude Request filters out any remaining empty rows with a defensive filter.

### Bot mention detection failing silently
**Symptom:** Bruce doesn't respond in mention-only channels even when @mentioned.
**Cause:** Using \$env.DISCORD_BOT_USER_ID which silently returns undefined in n8n expressions.
**Fix:** Hardcode bot user ID 1495252972026859520 everywhere. Same for guild ID 1495249842778148954. Never use \$env for these.

### Persist Assistant Message reading wrong data
**Symptom:** Assistant replies stored as {success: true} instead of actual content.
**Cause:** Node was reading \$json (output of Persist User Message = {success: true}) instead of reaching back to Parse Claude Reply.
**Fix:** Always use \$('Parse Claude Reply').first().json for the assistant content.

### Auto memory extraction not saving (scope='auto' rejected)
**Symptom:** Memory extraction pipeline runs end-to-end but INSERT fails silently.
**Cause:** Postgres CHECK constraint only allowed scope='user' and scope='channel'. The 'auto' value was rejected.
**Fix:** Migration 006 drops and recreates both check constraints to allow 'user', 'channel', and 'auto'. Insert Auto Memory node now has ON CONFLICT (discord_user_id, LOWER(content)) DO NOTHING.

### n8n dbTime.getTime log spam
**Symptom:** Container logs flooded with "TypeError: dbTime.getTime is not a function" from WaitTracker.
**Cause:** n8n :latest pulled a version with a Postgres timestamp compatibility bug.
**Fix:** Pin n8n version in docker-compose.yml via N8N_VERSION env var. Currently pinned to 2.16.1.

### Watchtower silently upgraded n8n, breaking rollback
**Symptom:** After running \`docker compose up -d --build discord-relay\`, n8n login failed with \`column User.role does not exist\`.
**Cause:** Watchtower had auto-upgraded n8n from 1.84.3 → 2.16.1, which ran DB migrations that renamed \`role\` → \`roleSlug\` (with FK). The compose up re-pulled the \`1.84.3\` tag (which Docker Hub had updated to a new build) and reverted the container, but the DB schema was now incompatible with 1.84.3's entity model.
**Fix:** Removed watchtower entirely. Pinned N8N_VERSION=2.16.1 in .env. All service updates are now manual. \`docker compose up -d --build <service>\` re-pulls other service images too — always confirm version pins before running it.

### Node 18 fetch fails with localhost on VPS
**Symptom:** Scripts using fetch('http://localhost:5678/...') fail with "fetch failed".
**Cause:** Ubuntu's Node 18 package has a known issue resolving localhost.
**Fix:** Always use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost. Example: N8N_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5678.

### Replicate API auth failure
**Symptom:** /image command returns 401.
**Cause:** Replicate uses Token prefix, not Bearer.
**Fix:** Auth header in Call Replicate node must be "Token {{ \$env.REPLICATE_API_TOKEN }}", not "Bearer".

### Thread replies going to parent channel
**Symptom:** Command responses (/search, /image, etc.) appear in the parent channel when used inside a thread.
**Cause:** Reply nodes were using Channel Router's channelId which points to the parent. Thread replies need to go to threadId.
**Fix:** All Discord reply nodes must use: {{ thread_id || channelId }} pattern.

### Env var access in n8n
Three things must be true for \$env.VAR_NAME to work in n8n:
1. Variable exists in .env file
2. Variable is mapped in docker-compose.yml under the n8n service's environment: block
3. N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE is set to "false"
Missing any one of these causes silent failure — \$env returns undefined with no error.

// Auto-search: inject Perplexity results if auto-search ran upstream.
let autoSearchBlock = '';
try {
  const autoSearchData = $('Detect Search Intent').first().json;
  if (autoSearchData && autoSearchData.needs_search) {
    const perplexityRes = $('Auto-Search Perplexity').first().json;
    let searchAnswer = '';
    try { searchAnswer = perplexityRes.choices[0].message.content || ''; } catch(_) {}
    if (searchAnswer) {
      autoSearchBlock = `\n\n<auto_search_results>
The following web search was automatically performed for context:
Query: ${autoSearchData.search_query}
Results: ${searchAnswer}
Use these results to inform your answer. Cite specific details naturally.
</auto_search_results>`;
    }
  }
} catch(_) {}

const router = $('Channel Router').first().json;
const isShared = router.historyScope === 'channel';

const historyRows = $('Fetch Conversation History').all()
  .map(i => i.json)
  .reverse()
  .filter(r => r && (r.role === 'user' || r.role === 'assistant'))
  .filter(r => r.content != null && String(r.content).trim().length > 0);
const memoryRows  = $input.all()
  .map(i => i.json)
  .filter(r => r && r.content);

// Memory block — injected into system prompt if there are any.
let memoryBlock = '';
if (memoryRows.length) {
  const bullets = memoryRows.map(r => `- ${r.content}`).join('\n');
  memoryBlock = `\n\n<long_term_memory>\nThe user has asked you to remember these facts. Use them in your reply when relevant; do not quote them back verbatim unless asked.\n${bullets}\n</long_term_memory>`;
}

// Shared-channel note: tell Claude that user turns are prefixed.
const sharedNote = isShared
  ? `\n\n<channel_context>\nThis is a shared channel with multiple people. Each user turn is prefixed with the speaker's name (e.g. "Jake: ..."). Address people by name when appropriate. Your own replies are not prefixed.\n</channel_context>`
  : '';

// Current date/time in household timezone. Injected so Bruce always knows
// the day/time without the user having to say.
const now = new Date();
const dateStr = now.toLocaleString('en-US', {
  weekday: 'long', year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric',
  hour: 'numeric', minute: '2-digit',
  timeZone: 'America/New_York',
  timeZoneName: 'short',
});
const dateBlock = `\n\n<current_time>\nIt is currently ${dateStr}.\n</current_time>`;

// Current speaker: who Bruce is talking to RIGHT NOW. Injected on every
// run (shared or private channel) so Bruce always knows who he's
// addressing and never asks "who are you?" in a private channel.
const currentSpeakerBlock = `\n\n<current_speaker>\nYou are currently speaking with ${router.userName || 'someone'}. Address them by this name. Their Discord user ID is ${router.userId || 'unknown'}. Do not ask who they are — they have already been identified.\n</current_speaker>`;

const selfContextPrefix = SELF_CONTEXT_CHANNELS.includes(router.channelName) ? SELF_CONTEXT + '\n\n' : '';
const devContextPrefix = DEV_CONTEXT_CHANNELS.includes(router.channelName) ? DEV_CONTEXT + '\n\n' : '';
const systemPrompt = devContextPrefix + selfContextPrefix + (router.systemPrompt || '') + currentSpeakerBlock + memoryBlock + sharedNote + dateBlock + autoSearchBlock;

// Speaker prefix helper — pre-migration rows have NULL discord_username, so
// fall back to a short user-id tag.
function labelUser(r) {
  const name = r.discord_username
    || (r.discord_user_id ? `user-${String(r.discord_user_id).slice(-4)}` : 'someone');
  return `${name}: ${r.content}`;
}

const messages = historyRows.map(r => {
  if (isShared && r.role === 'user') {
    return { role: 'user', content: labelUser(r) };
  }
  return { role: r.role, content: r.content };
});

// Pull image/PDF content blocks forwarded by Merge Attachment Branches.
// Empty array when the user didn't upload anything (false branch of Has
// Attachments injects content_blocks: []).
let contentBlocks = [];
try {
  const merged = $('Merge Attachment Branches').first().json;
  contentBlocks = Array.isArray(merged && merged.content_blocks) ? merged.content_blocks : [];
} catch (_) { /* merge node absent — ignore */ }

const userText = router.content || '';
const fallbackText = 'Please analyze the attached file(s).';
const baseText = userText.trim().length > 0
  ? userText
  : (contentBlocks.length > 0 ? fallbackText : '');

// In shared channels, prefix the CURRENT user's turn too so Claude can
// attribute the live question.
const currentText = (isShared && baseText.length > 0)
  ? `${router.userName || 'someone'}: ${baseText}`
  : baseText;

// When attachments are present, Anthropic expects a content-block array
// (images/docs first, text last). Otherwise keep the legacy string form so
// downstream shape is unchanged for the text-only path.
const userContent = contentBlocks.length > 0
  ? [...contentBlocks, { type: 'text', text: currentText }]
  : currentText;

messages.push({ role: 'user', content: userContent });

// Defense in depth: drop any message whose content is empty/whitespace.
// The Anthropic API rejects the whole request with `messages.N: user
// messages must have non-empty content` if even one is bad, so a single
// legacy empty row from before the contentForPersist fallback was in
// place would poison the run. Handles both the string-content form and
// the content-block-array form (where we require at least one non-empty
// text block or a non-text block like an image).
const filteredMessages = messages.filter(m => {
  if (typeof m.content === 'string') {
    return m.content.trim().length > 0;
  }
  if (Array.isArray(m.content) && m.content.length > 0) {
    return m.content.some(b =>
      (b.type !== 'text') || (typeof b.text === 'string' && b.text.trim().length > 0)
    );
  }
  return false;
});

const droppedCount = messages.length - filteredMessages.length;
if (droppedCount > 0) {
  console.log(`[build-claude] dropped ${droppedCount} empty message(s) from history`);
}

return [{
  json: {
    ...router,
    memoryCount: memoryRows.length,
    attachmentCount: contentBlocks.length,
    historyRowCount: historyRows.length,
    droppedEmptyMessages: droppedCount,
    requestBody: {
      model: router.model || $env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
      max_tokens: 1024,
      system: systemPrompt,
      messages: filteredMessages,
    },
  }
}];

INSERT INTO discord_conversations
  (message_id, discord_user_id, discord_username, channel_id, channel_name, thread_id, thread_name, role, content)
SELECT
  '{{ $json.messageId }}',
  '{{ $json.userId }}',
  '{{ ($json.userName || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}',
  '{{ $json.channelId }}',
  '{{ $json.channelName }}',
  {{ $json.isThread ? "'" + $json.threadId + "'"     : 'NULL' }},
  {{ $json.isThread ? "'" + $json.threadName + "'"   : 'NULL' }},
  'user',
  '{{ ($json.contentForPersist || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}'
WHERE length(trim('{{ ($json.contentForPersist || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}')) > 0
ON CONFLICT (message_id) DO UPDATE
  SET content = EXCLUDED.content,
      discord_username = EXCLUDED.discord_username
  WHERE length(trim(EXCLUDED.content)) > 0;

INSERT INTO discord_conversations
  (message_id, discord_user_id, discord_username, channel_id, channel_name, thread_id, thread_name, role, content)
VALUES
  ('{{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.messageId }}_reply',
   '{{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.userId }}',
   'Bruce',
   '{{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.channelId }}',
   '{{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.channelName }}',
   {{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.isThread ? "'" + $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.threadId + "'"     : 'NULL' }},
   {{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.isThread ? "'" + $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.threadName + "'" : 'NULL' }},
   'assistant',
   '{{ $('Parse Claude Reply').first().json.reply.replace(/'/g, "''") }}')
ON CONFLICT (message_id) DO UPDATE
  SET content = EXCLUDED.content,
      discord_username = EXCLUDED.discord_username;

DELETE FROM discord_conversations
WHERE {{ $json.isThread ? "thread_id = '" + $json.threadId + "'" : "channel_id = '" + $json.channelId + "' AND thread_id IS NULL" }};

INSERT INTO user_memories
  (discord_user_id, scope, channel_id, channel_name, content)
SELECT
  '{{ $json.userId }}',
  'auto',
  '{{ $json.memoryScopeId }}',
  '{{ ($json.memoryScopeName || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}',
  '{{ ($json.memoryContent || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}'
WHERE length(trim('{{ ($json.memoryContent || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}')) > 0
ON CONFLICT (discord_user_id, LOWER(content)) DO NOTHING;

FROM recipes
WHERE discord_user_id = '{{ $('Channel Router').first().json.userId }}'
{{ $('Channel Router').first().json.recipesQuery
   ? "AND (LOWER(title) LIKE LOWER('%" + $('Channel Router').first().json.recipesQuery + "%') OR LOWER(body) LIKE LOWER('%" + $('Channel Router').first().json.recipesQuery + "%'))"
   : '' }}
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 20;
